response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack and systems developer, but misses several hard requirements for this role: no Japanese language proficiency (native-level required), only brief professional C experience (~6 months at National Instruments versus the required strong C for UNIX), and no evidence of Linux kernel analysis or modification, embedded networking, L2/L3 switch development, or security technologies like MACsec or 802.1X. His strengths lie in web, cloud, and application development, which do not transfer to embedded LAN-switch firmware security work.
strengths: Long, varied engineering career including some low-level work (C/C++ at National Instruments, low-latency DLang services)
Strong software craftsmanship practices: TDD, CI/CD, testing tools authorship
Solid Linux and infrastructure familiarity (Docker, Kubernetes, CI servers)
concerns: No Japanese language ability listed — a hard requirement for the role
No embedded networking, switch, or security-protocol experience (MACsec, 802.1X, SNMP/NETCONF)
Minimal recent C experience and no Linux kernel source-modification background

key_factors.factor: General seniority and low-level systems exposure
